name: Joe Carroll
abstract: Joe "Bebop" Carroll (November 25, 1919 – February 1, 1981) was an American jazz vocalist who worked with Dizzy Gillespie between 1949 and 1953. His collaborations with Gillespie include the humorous songs "Swing Low, Sweet Cadillac" and "Oo Bla Dee."

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dizzy Gillespie: Genius at Midnight

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I made an early record with Dizzy on his DG label with Joe Carroll and Dizzy. Joe was in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I remembered Joe Carroll in Toledo was singing with Dizzy, which was my gig, I was supposed to be Dizzy’s first singer... So I called Joe and I said, “where is Bird?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: I hooked up with Joe... I used Joe and (?) together a couple of times. But mostly I used Jimmy Roberts on piano with Joe.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I called Joe Carroll... But I liked Joe. He was a beautiful guy. So I called him up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
